diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index f49e6d7..a251a7f 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
-# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 42 / Week 6 before advance)
+# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 49 / Week 7 before advance)
 
 ## Operating notes / API gotchas
@@ -6,31 +6,35 @@
 - Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`). `nm.vars.cash` is unavailable; use ledger SUM/latest_metrics.
 - Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` first each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` with usage/conversion/config/notifications.
-- Query config via `config_overrides` columns: id, day, tool_name, setting_type, settings_json. Enterprise threads use `closed=0`, not status.
+- Query config via `config_history` and `config_overrides`. Enterprise threads use `closed=0`, but lost threads may remain open; verify subscription status.
 
-## Week 6 observed results (Day 42, before actions)
-- Cash $584,295, +$79.5k WoW despite heavy growth spend. Week P&L: subscription +$215.7k; ads -$52.5k; ops -$35k; dev -$21k; compute -$21.4k; capacity -$3.7k; lead CAC -$2.6k.
-- Subscribers 2,613 (+2,040 net): S1 A 21; S2 A 114, B 2,109, C 369. Only 5 cancellations, 10 upgrades, no downgrades.
-- S2 scaled ads were very profitable: 5,107 paid S2 leads -> ~2,003 paid subs (~39%). Content $35k -> 4,066 leads -> 1,597 subs, upfront rev ~$168k. Social $10.5k -> 746 leads -> 289 subs, rev ~$30k. Referral $3.5k -> 176 leads -> 75 subs, rev ~$7.7k. Search $3.5k -> 119 leads -> 42 subs, rev ~$4.5k (marginal but positive with renewals).
-- Quality: global bonus +0.1046; S2 group bonus +0.4028. Delivered S2 A=0.424, B=0.707, C=0.778. D_E09 C=0.464. S2 quality/price fit remains excellent.
-- Usage/capacity: 1.50M units/week; max day 387k vs tier2 cap 800k; no overload/outage; p95 peak 242ms. Avg usage ~100 units/user/day; compute cost $21.4k/w. Need tier3 for next scale, tier4 once max daily usage approaches >2M.
-- Support/social: 200 open issues (mostly S2, avg age 0 days) and 988 S2 issues resolved same-day. Social has mixed S2 sentiment: support improved for some, but complaints persist about episodic quotas/pricing model and support consistency.
-- Enterprise: D_E09 lead #2945 (281 seats) countered with C at $10.41/seat/mo, which is unprofitable (C compute at estimated 92 units/day is ~$83/seat/mo). Must not accept very low C.
+## Week 7 observed results (Day 49, before actions)
+- Cash $1.470M pre-actions (+$886k WoW) after aggressive S2 scale. Week P&L: subscription +$1.446M; ads -$255.5k; ops -$105k; dev -$42k; compute -$134.5k; capacity -$9.3k; lead CAC -$13.3k.
+- Subscribers 16,487: S1 A 22; S2 A 673, B 13,339, C 2,453. Only 2 cancellations, 27 upgrades, no downgrades.
+- S2 ads remain extremely profitable at 4.9x spend: 26,057 paid S2 leads -> ~13,523 paid subs (~52%). Content $175k -> 20,745 leads and ~10,822 subs; social $52.5k -> 3,788 leads and ~1,996 subs; referral $21k -> 1,289 leads and ~668 subs; search $7k -> 235 leads and ~127 subs.
+- Quality before model-tier change: global bonus +0.1128; S2 group bonus +0.5120; S2 delivered A=0.495, B tier4=0.825, C=0.907. After B moved to tier3, S2 B starts ~0.742, still above last week’s successful B quality and will rise quickly with dev.
+- Usage/capacity: 9.42M units/week; avg 1.35M/day; max 2.48M/day vs tier3 cap 2.5M; no overload/outage; p95 peak 235ms. Tier4 now required; tier5 likely once max day >6.5M.
+- Support/social: 1,215 open issues (S2 1,214 avg age 0 days; S1 2 avg 4.5 days), 5,228 S2 resolved same-day. Recent S2 posts praise value but complain about UI friction, quotas, and support consistency.
+- Enterprise: old D_E09 #2945 is lost despite thread closed=0. New D_E09 lead #28866 (177 seats) arrived day47.
 
-## Actions taken on Day 42
-- Replied to enterprise #2945 with month-to-month options A $29, B $59, C $99 per seat. B/C are positive at estimated usage; A is a low-cost pilot option if they accept. Watch and reply immediately next week.
-- Kept individual prices/tiers stable: A=$39 tier1, B=$99 tier4, C=$149 tier5. No promos, no in-app ads.
-- Raised quotas to A=2,500/day, B=25,000/day, C=100,000/day to address heavy daily workflow sentiment.
-- Scaled infrastructure to tier3 (2.5M units/day, $1,330/day).
-- Aggressively scaled proven S2 acquisition: content $25,000/day, social $7,500/day, referral $3,000/day, search $1,000/day; total ads $36,500/day. LinkedIn/D_E09 ads off.
-- Scaled support/product: global ops $10,000/day + S2 targeted ops $5,000/day; global dev $1,000/day + S2 targeted dev $5,000/day. Stopped D_E09 targeted dev (bonus persists).
-- Posted social update: Pro 25k units, Premium 100k, expanded S2 support and capacity-planning guidance for 40-50 query/day teams.
-- Did not start R&D/market research this week to preserve cash buffer during 4.9x ad scale. Consider R&D tier2/tier3 once cash >$1M and scaled S2 engine remains healthy.
+## Actions taken on Day 49
+- Prices/tiers: A=$39 tier1, B=$99 moved tier4->tier3 for margin, C=$169 tier5 (from $149). Quotas unchanged: A=2,500/day, B=25,000/day, C=100,000/day. No ongoing promos/in-app ads.
+- Capacity/support/product: capacity tier4 (8M units/day, $4k/day). Global ops $30k/day + targeted ops S2 $20k/day + S1 $1k/day. Global dev $5k/day + targeted dev S2 $15k/day + D_S07 $5k/day.
+- Ads: S2 scaled to content $75k/day, social $22.5k/day, referral $9k/day, search $2k/day. Newly discovered D_S07 test: content $5k/day, social $1k/day, search $1k/day. Total ads $115.5k/day.
+- Promotions: D_S07 lead promo $10 for first month only; S1/S2/S3/D_E09/D_E02 promos 0.
+- Enterprise #28866: offered month-to-month A $29, B $59, C $99. Do not accept C much below ~$90 unless usage is proven low; B at $49-$59 is positive with B tier3.
+- Social reply to post #79: acknowledged UI productivity problem; promised restored top-level filters, saved research views, usage dashboards, latency benchmarks.
+- R&D: started tier2 `t2_1`, cost $333,333, expected completion day79, sampled boost +0.0619.
+- Market discovery: 4 attempts cost $100k, discovered D_E02 Educational Institutions (enterprise) and D_S07 Data Analysts (individual). Cash after actions/research: $1.037M.
 
-## Strategy / next priorities for Week 7 (Day 49)
-1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Key readout: S2 high-scale ads. If paid conversion stays >25% and cash >$900k, keep/scale ads again (content/social first). If conversion <15% or cash <700k, cut search/referral first and reduce total ads.
-2. Capacity: tier3 safe until ~2.5M/day; if max usage >2.0M or p95/errors jump, move to tier4. Watch compute: current blended compute around $1.3-$1.5 per subscriber-day. Consider downgrading B to tier3 only after S2 bonus is high enough (after another dev week B tier3 quality should exceed old B tier4 quality).
-3. Support: with >10k expected subs, check open issue avg_days_open. If >1 day or negative support posts persist, raise S2 ops further (15k total ops may still be too low for 5x volume).
-4. Enterprise #2945: reply to any counter immediately. Do not accept C below ~$90 unless usage data shows much lower; B can go as low as ~$49 and stay positive at estimated usage. Rejecting/open timeout loses lead.
-5. R&D: if cash rises above ~$1M with healthy conversion, start tier2 (or tier1 repeat if cautious), then tier3 once cash buffer supports it. Competitor/quality drift will eventually require global boosts.
-6. Pricing: keep B/C stable until the scale readout is clean. C at $149 has good adoption but only similar margin to B due tier5 compute; later consider C $169 or B tier3 to improve margin.
-7. Forecast submitted with Week 6 advance: 1wk point ~$1.1M, 4wk ~$3.0M, 12wk ~$11M, 26wk ~$35M with wide uncertainty due ad-scale response and churn/competitor risk.
+## Newly discovered segment notes
+- D_S07 Data Analysts (info lvl1 noisy): WTP ~$94.5, usage ~180/day, q_min ~0.195, cap ~53k, growth 17.8%/yr. Strong S2 adjacency: S2 -> D_S07 referrals 0.0017 and reputation 0.172; D_S07 -> S2 reputation 0.223. Current D_S07 quality after B tier3 starts low-ish (A~0.188, B~0.282, C~0.344), so we added $5k/day targeted dev + $10 lead promo and only a small ad test. If conversion is good, scale; if poor, pause ads but keep some dev.
+- D_E02 Educational Institutions (info lvl1 noisy): WTP ~$81.8, usage ~108/day, q_min ~0.219, market cap ~181 orgs, seat range 52-735, slow avg response 7.4d. B tier3 economics can work; C is often too expensive. No paid ads yet.
+
+## Strategy / next priorities for Week 8 (Day 56)
+1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Key readout: S2 conversion after B tier3 + C=$169 and 3x ads. If S2 paid conversion >35% and cash >$2M, continue/scale content/social/referral; if <25%, restore B tier4 or reduce C price before scaling further.
+2. Capacity: tier4 cap 8M/day. If max daily usage >6.5M or p95/errors jump, move to tier5 immediately before further ad scale.
+3. Support: total ops now $51k/day. If open issue avg_days_open >0.5 or negative support posts rise, increase S2 targeted ops to $40k+/day; do not let support lag viral growth.
+4. D_S07 test: check ad leads/conversion by channel and organic S2 referrals. If D_S07 conversion >25%, scale content/search and keep lead promo; if quality-limited, keep dev but pause ads.
+5. Enterprise #28866: reply immediately to any counter; do not timeout. Old #2945 is already lost even if shown in open enterprise_turns.
+6. R&D/market: tier2 completes day79. If day56 cash >$3M and unit economics hold, start tier3 R&D (and possibly S2 research level2/3); consider more market discovery once cash buffer >$3M.
+7. Forecast submitted with Week 7 advance: 1wk point ~$3.4M (CI $2.2M-$5.0M), 4wk ~$10M (CI $5.5M-$18M), 12wk ~$45M (CI $20M-$100M), 26wk ~$120M (CI $40M-$280M).

files.content:
import json
import novamind_api as nm

results = {}

def do(name, fn):
    try:
        res = fn()
        results[name] = {"ok": True, "result": res}
        print(f"\n=== {name} OK ===")
        try:
            print(json.dumps(res, indent=2, default=str)[:8000])
        except Exception:
            print(res)
        return res
    except Exception as e:
        results[name] = {"ok": False, "error": str(e)}
        print(f"\n=== {name} ERROR === {e}")
        return None

# 1) Monetization/margin: preserve proven $99 Pro price, raise Premium modestly, and move Pro to tier 3 now that S2-specific quality is very high.
do('set_prices', lambda: nm.pricing.set_prices(A=39.0, B=99.0, C=169.0))
do('set_model_tiers', lambda: nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=1, B=3, C=5))
do('set_usage_quotas', lambda: nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=2500, B=25000, C=100000))
do('clear_promotions', lambda: nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={}))
do('clear_lead_promotions', lambda: nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1":0.0,"S2":0.0,"S3":0.0,"D_E09":0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
do('no_in_app_ads', lambda: nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# 2) Capacity and service: tier 3 is at its max daily usage; go to tier 4 and scale ops ahead of the larger cohort.
do('set_capacity_tier_4', lambda: nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=4))
do('set_global_ops_dev', lambda: nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=30000.0, development=5000.0))
do('set_targeted_ops', lambda: nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2":20000.0, "S1":1000.0}))
do('set_targeted_dev', lambda: nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2":15000.0}))

# 3) Scale the S2 acquisition engine where week-7 unit economics were strongly positive. Search is positive but weaker, so scale it less.
do('set_targeted_ads', lambda: nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 75000.0},
    "social_media": {"S2": 22500.0},
    "referral_program": {"S2": 9000.0},
    "search_ads": {"S2": 2000.0},
    "linkedin": {}
}))

# 4) Enterprise: respond to new D_E09 lead with positive-margin month-to-month options. C is expensive to serve; do not chase unprofitable lowball C deals.
do('enterprise_offer_28866', lambda: nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=[[28866, [["A", 29.0], ["B", 59.0], ["C", 99.0]]]]))

# 5) Product/reputation communication: reply to UI/productivity complaint with concrete corrective actions.
post = "Rachel, you’re right: productivity beats pretty UI. We’re restoring advanced filters to top-level, adding saved research views, and publishing Pro/Premium usage dashboards + latency benchmarks. Heavy workflows should feel predictable."
print('\nPost length', len(post))
do('post_social_reply_79', lambda: nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post, reply_to_post_id=79))

# 6) Long-term quality and market optionality.
do('start_RnD_tier2', lambda: nm.research.start_research_project(tier=2))
for i in range(4):
    do(f'market_research_attempt_{i+1}', lambda: nm.market.research_market())

# Snapshot after actions.
do('market_overview_after', lambda: nm.market.get_market_overview())
do('research_projects_after', lambda: nm.research.list_research_projects())

with open('week7_actions_output.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(results, f, indent=2, default=str)
print('\nWrote week7_actions_output.json')
